summary: High‑quality soundboard recording of a late‑71 show highlighted by a smoking Dark Star → Sittin’ On Top Of The World → Dark Star sandwich, strong Cumberland and Jack Straw, with a crisp mix that sometimes lets Keith’s piano dominate.
review: The performance shines with an extended Dark Star medley, a fiery Cumberland Blues, the second‑ever Jack Straw and a memorable St. Stephen encore, all backed by tight rhythm and inspired interplay between Jerry and newcomer Keith.  The soundboard source is generally clear and “album‑ready,” though a few listeners note that Keith’s piano can be a bit forward in the mix.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and praise both the musical set (standout Dark Star sandwich, strong late‑71 repertoire) and the recording quality; occasional mix complaints lower the rating only slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp, album/CD‑ready sound with excellent balance, but Keith’s piano is sometimes overly forward, making Jerry’s guitar a bit buried for some listeners.

standout_songs: Dark Star > Sittin' On Top Of The World > Dark Star
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows around a classic Dark Star sandwich, delivering high energy early (Cumberland, Jack Straw) and closing with an uplifting St. Stephen encore; the overall pacing feels tight and exhilarating.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone and improvisational interplay with Keith are repeatedly praised as hypnotic and inspired.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano adds a fresh, electrified texture that many love; a few reviewers feel it sits too high in the mix, occasionally masking Jerry’s lead.
